title: DIY SUMMER WEATHERIZATION WORKSHOPS - 211info
text: COMMUNITY ENERGY PROJECT DIY SUMMER WEATHERIZATION WORKSHOPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Teaches low-cost cooling and energy saving techniques for the home. Includes tips for saving water and energy, cooling homes without using air conditioning, and preventing heat-related illness. Qualified participants receive a free kit of materials to lower energy costs and stay cooler in their home during the warmer months. 2705 E Burnside Street Suite 112 Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Workshops: Unrestricted, but free supplies are restricted to households earning no more than 80% median family income Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9:30am-5pm. Workshop dates vary. Email (503) 284-6827 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 284-6827 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website, call, or email for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus 20 stops at 28th and E Burnside Street Services: Subject Specific Public Awareness/Education * Energy Conservation Issues Weatherization Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - WEATHERIZATION AND LEAD WORKSHOPS UTILITIES - LOW INCOME COMMUNITY SOLAR If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
